2605.18897.title: Cross-Subject Intracranial EEG Reconstruction from Scalp Recordings Using Multi-Scale Cross-Attention Transformers
2605.18897.paper_summary: Intracranial EEG (iEEG) provides high-fidelity neural recordings essential for clinical and brain-computer interface applications, but acquiring these signals requires invasive surgery. While recent studies have attempted to estimate iEEG from non-invasive scalp EEG, most rely on patient-specific models, creating a circular dependency: if surgery is required to collect training data, the non-invasive model offers limited practical benefit. In this study, we address the challenge of cross-subject iEEG reconstruction by predicting intracranial signals for unseen patients using models trained on other individuals. We propose CAST (Cross-Attention Spatial-Temporal Transformer), a machine learning framework that translates scalp EEG into multi-channel iEEG waveforms through a two-stage transfer learning strategy. First, a temporal encoder extracts multi-scale neural representations at three different resolutions. Then, because electrode placements vary substantially across patients, a channel-aware decoder is calibrated using only a few minutes of data from the target subject. We evaluated the proposed method using leave-one-subject-out cross-validation on two public datasets comprising 1,282 iEEG channels. Experimental results demonstrate that CAST reconstructs cortical signals located near the scalp surface substantially better than deep subcortical activity. In highly observable sensorimotor regions, the model achieved peak correlations of up to r=0.864 in the precentral gyrus. Furthermore, with a channel selection strategy, CAST obtained a mean correlation of r=0.545 on viable subjects, outperforming previous within-subject baselines. These findings indicate that cortical iEEG signals can be reconstructed for unseen subjects from scalp EEG without extensive patient-specific training, and that only a brief calibration phase is sufficient to adapt the model to new hardware configurations.
2605.18897.paper_summary_zh: 顱內腦電圖 (iEEG) 提供了對臨床和腦-電腦介面應用至關重要的高保真神經記錄，但獲取這些信號需要侵入性手術。雖然最近的研究已試圖從非侵入性頭皮 EEG 估計 iEEG，但大多數依賴於病人特定模型，形成了一種循環依賴：如果需要手術來收集訓練數據，則非侵入性模型的實際效益有限。在本研究中，我們通過使用在其他個體上訓練的模型來預測未見患者的顱內信號，解決了跨主題 iEEG 重建的挑戰。我們提出了 CAST（跨注意力空間-時間變換器），這是一個機器學習框架，通過兩階段轉移學習策略將頭皮 EEG 轉換為多通道 iEEG 波形。首先，時間編碼器在三種不同的解析度下提取多尺度神經表示。然後，由於電極位置在患者之間變化很大，因此通道感知解碼器僅使用目標主題的幾分鐘數據進行校準。我們使用留一主題交叉驗證對兩個包含 1,282 個 iEEG 通道的公共數據集評估了所提出的方法。實驗結果表明，CAST 在重建位於頭皮表面附近的皮層信號方面，顯著優於深層皮下活動。在高度可觀察的感覺運動區域，該模型在前中央回達到了高達 r=0.864 的峰值相關性。此外，通過通道選擇策略，CAST 在可行主題上獲得了平均相關性 r=0.545，超越了先前的主題內基準。這些發現表明，皮層 iEEG 信號可以從頭皮 EEG 中重建未見主題，而無需大量病人特定的訓練，並且僅需短暫的校準階段即可使模型適應新的硬件配置。
2605.18897.author: Tien-Dat Pham et.al.
